name: Broccoli Saute with Pine Nuts
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Artichoke hearts, drained and halved
1/8 teaspoon Ground chili peppers or cayenne pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Select a converted or par-cooked rice that will cook in about 10 minutes.
2. Note on tomato: This recipe requires ripe and firm tomatoes (like restaurant waxed); alternatively, you can French fry the cut tomatoes by hand.
3. Heat oil in a skillet and saute broccoli florets and nuts for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning of the nuts.
4. Use a slotted spoon to remove the broccoli and nuts; set aside.
5. Add butter to the skillet. Using the metal blade of a food processor, drop garlic through the feed tube with the motor running until minced.
6. Add garlic and rice to the butter in the skillet and saute about 2 minutes until the rice is golden.
7. Stir in stock and simmer for about 10 minutes until the rice is tender. Add more stock if needed to cook the rice.
8. Remove the skillet from heat. While the rice cooks, process the tomatoes into strips using a French fry disk.
9. Drain off excess liquid and add the tomatoes, broccoli, nuts, raisins, and artichoke hearts to the skillet. Toss over medium heat until the vegetables are heated through.
10. Season with salt, pepper, and cayenne pepper to taste. Serve immediately. Makes 6 servings.
Variation: Substitute slivered almonds for pine nuts. Omit the artichokes.
